title: fagraea floribunda
intro: Fagraea floribunda, also known as the 'Frangipani of the Woods', is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is a shrub or small tree that is native to tropical Asia and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Fagraea floribunda include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Fagraea floribunda can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms for therapeutic purposes.
